seoTitle: How to Create, Start, and Complete a Sprint in Jira
metaDescription: A full walkthrough of the sprint lifecycle in Jira, from creating and planning a sprint to starting it and closing it out when work is done.
intro: Running a sprint in Jira involves three distinct stages: creating it, starting it with a defined scope, and completing it once the work is done. This guide walks through the full lifecycle for teams new to Scrum in Jira.

steps.heading: Create a new sprint
steps.body: From your backlog view, create a new sprint, which starts out empty and ready to be populated with work.

steps.heading: Add issues to the sprint
steps.body: Select issues from your backlog and add them to the sprint you just created, based on what your team plans to tackle.

steps.heading: Start the sprint
steps.body: Set a sprint duration and start it, which moves the selected issues onto the active board for your team to work through.

steps.heading: Track progress during the sprint
steps.body: As work moves forward, monitor the board to see issues progressing through the columns you've configured.

steps.heading: Complete the sprint
steps.body: Once the sprint duration ends, close it out. Jira will show you what was completed and let you decide where to move any unfinished issues.

faq.question: What happens to unfinished issues when I complete a sprint?
faq.answer: Jira will prompt you to choose whether unfinished issues go back to the backlog or roll into the next sprint.

faq.question: Can I add issues to a sprint after it's already started?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add issues to an active sprint, though doing so frequently can make it harder to track original sprint commitments.

faq.question: How long should a sprint typically last?
faq.answer: Sprint length varies by team, but one to two weeks is common, and Jira lets you set whatever duration fits your process.

faq.question: Do I need a Scrum board to run sprints?
faq.answer: Yes, sprints are a feature tied to Scrum-style boards in Jira, so your project needs to be configured with a Scrum board to use them.
